IERC20","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"custom:security-note":"First checkout produces exactly two subscriber signatures:        1. USDC.approve(subscriptionManagerAddress, amount * 12)  // one year        2. subscribe(...)                                          // first period into escrow      Renewals produce zero subscriber signatures. Revoking the allowance from      the wallet effectively cancels future renewals.","details":"Architecture (per the Subscription Protocol implementation addendum):      - FIRST payments and trial conversions are held in per-subscription escrow        until settlementDeadline passes; the settlement window is the ONLY refund        path. After the window closes, settlePeriod() PUSHES the merchant share        directly to merchantPayout and the fee to platformTreasury in one tx.      - All renewals are allowance-based: renewFromAllowance() pulls from the        subscriber's pre-approved USDC allowance and pushes the split immediately.        Merchants never sign a claim transaction \u2014 there is no claimFunds().      - The platform (owner) is the arbiter: it signs settlePeriod(),        renewFromAllowance(), and refund() calls from the off-chain billing engine.      - refund() operates ONLY on escrowBalance. Funds already pushed to a        merchant cannot be recovered by this contract.","errors":{"EnforcedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is paused."}],"ExpectedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is not paused."}],"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"subscribe(bytes32,address,bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"amount":"USDC amount per cycle (6 decimals: 9_000_000 = $9.00)","interval":"Billing cycle length in seconds (2_592_000 = 30 days)","merchantPayout":"Verified merchant payout address (Circle user-controlled or external)","planId":"bytes32 plan identifier (off-chain plan reference)","settlementWindow":"Escrow hold in seconds (per-plan; default 24h for first payments)","subId":"Unique bytes32 ID generated by the platform off-chain","trialDuration":"Trial length in seconds. The frontend should prompt the subscriber to revoke the USDC allowance."},"isDueBilling(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns true when a subscription is billable and past its nextBillingDate.         Used by the billing engine to build its renewal batch."},"isDueSettlement(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns true when escrowed funds are past their settlement deadline.         Used by the billing engine to build its settlement batch."},"platformFeeBps()":{"notice":"Platform fee in basis points (100 = 1%, max 1000 = 10%)"},"platformTreasury()":{"notice":"Wallet that receives the platform's share of every payment"},"refund(bytes32,uint8)":{"notice":"Issue a pro-rated refund to the subscriber. Arbiter-only.         Operates ONLY on escrowBalance \u2014 funds already pushed to the         merchant cannot be recovered without merchant cooperation."},"renewFromAllowance(bytes32)":{"notice":"Process a recurring renewal (or trial conversion) from the         subscriber's pre-approved USDC allowance. Arbiter-only.         Trial conversions are the subscription's FIRST payment, so they are         pulled into escrow and held for the settlement window. Standard         renewals are split and PUSHED to merchantPayout immediately.         Emits PaymentFailed (and returns false without reverting) when the         subscriber has insufficient allowance or balance \u2014 the billing         engine then enters the retry cycle (daily \u00d7 7) before cancelling."},"settlePeriod(bytes32)":{"notice":"Release escrowed funds once the settlement window has closed. If trialDuration > 0, no payment is taken until the trial ends; the         billing engine then calls renewFromAllowance(), and that trial         conversion is escrowed for the same window."},"subscribeWithPermit((bytes32,address,address,bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint8,bytes32,bytes32))":{"notice":"Gasless activation. Platform-only: the subscriber signs ONLY an         EIP-2612 permit off-chain (one signature, no gas); the platform         submits this tx and pays Arc gas. The permit grants the recurring         allowance, then the first period is pulled into settlement-window         escrow from the subscriber's Arc USDC. Cross-chain checkouts mint to         the subscriber via CCTP off-chain, then activate through this path."},"usdc()":{"notice":"USDC token contract on Arc"}},"notice":"On-chain subscription billing contract for SweepConsole. s/introspection/IERC165.sol":{"keccak256":"0x8891738ffe910f0cf2da09566928589bf5d63f4524dd734fd9cedbac3274dd5c","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://971f954442df5c2ef5b5ebf1eb245d7105d9fbacc7386ee5c796df1d45b21617","dweb:/ipfs/QmadRjHbkicwqwwh61raUEapaVEtaLMcYbQZWs9gUkgj3u"]},"src/SubscriptionManager.sol":{"keccak256":"0xf71bffd2a2d9b777b62285179e403b72884d4a93193b6508616c8c972816fa78","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://2ce12986f8dc7a9659985fd029a57bb10ffb0181e45c1657bd67a797a6a2fc45","dweb:/ipfs/QmWSp72idjALbS2m38uR7Xg4RCrxsq6G9pEY8rG6FatxCB"]},"src/interfaces/ISubscriptionManager.sol":{"keccak256":"0xbae0f456da5c996bbe724190ca93948a026f4c234a1f50335b009e8b1126ab27","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://cbb6a7fa11bc7b5dbd9ceb0af5ad604c3a7f3b9075cf2c11833cd02da1dc6bf4","dweb:/ipfs/QmdbkcRrCsEPtu28GRiDbi4frVrdSbavjsMEWEAweC5Euf"]}},"version":1}

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

params.refundPct: Percentage of the escrowed amount to refund (1–100)

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

params.interval: Billing cycle length in seconds (2_592_000 = 30 days)
params.merchantPayout: Verified merchant payout address (Circle user-controlled or external)
params.settlementWindow: Escrow hold in seconds (per-plan; default 24h for first payments)
params.subId: Unique bytes32 ID generated by the platform off-chain
params.trialDuration: Trial length in seconds. Pass 0 for no trial.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

MAX_RETRIES().notice: Billing engine retries daily up to 7 times before cancelling

cancelSubscription(bytes32).notice: Cancel a subscription. Callable by the subscriber themselves or the platform.         Any USDC still held in escrow is returned to the subscriber in the same tx.         The frontend should prompt the subscriber to revoke the USDC allowance.

isDueBilling(bytes32).notice: Returns true when a subscription is billable and past its nextBillingDate.         Used by the billing engine to build its renewal batch.

isDueSettlement(bytes32).notice: Returns true when escrowed funds are past their settlement deadline.         Used by the billing engine to build its settlement batch.

platformFeeBps().notice: Platform fee in basis points (100 = 1%, max 1000 = 10%)

platformTreasury().notice: Wallet that receives the platform's share of every payment

refund(bytes32,uint8).notice: Issue a pro-rated refund to the subscriber. Arbiter-only.         Operates ONLY on escrowBalance — funds already pushed to the         merchant cannot be recovered without merchant cooperation.

renewFromAllowance(bytes32).notice: Process a recurring renewal (or trial conversion) from the         subscriber's pre-approved USDC allowance. Arbiter-only.         Trial conversions are the subscription's FIRST payment, so they are         pulled into escrow and held for the settlement window. Standard         renewals are split and PUSHED to merchantPayout immediately.         Emits PaymentFailed (and returns false without reverting) when the         subscriber has insufficient allowance or balance — the billing         engine then enters the retry cycle (daily × 7) before cancelling.

settlePeriod(bytes32).notice: Release escrowed funds once the settlement window has closed.         PUSHES the merchant share directly to merchantPayout and the         platform fee to platformTreasury in a single transaction — the         merchant never signs a claim.

subscribe(bytes32,address,bytes32,uint25.notice: Activate a new subscription.         Before calling, the subscriber must have called:           USDC.approve(address(this), amount * 12)   // one year allowance         If trialDuration == 0, the first period is pulled into escrow and         held until `settlementWindow` seconds pass (the refund window).         If trialDuration > 0, no payment is taken until the trial ends; the         billing engine then calls renewFromAllowance(), and that trial         conversion is escrowed for the same window.

subscribeWithPermit((bytes32,address,add.notice: Gasless activation. Platform-only: the subscriber signs ONLY an         EIP-2612 permit off-chain (one signature, no gas); the platform         submits this tx and pays Arc gas. The permit grants the recurring         allowance, then the first period is pulled into settlement-window         escrow from the subscriber's Arc USDC. Cross-chain checkouts mint to         the subscriber via CCTP off-chain, then activate through this path.
